gdpins 0.0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Getting Started with gdpins
    • Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
    • Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
  • Changelog

Create a fake (tempdir-backed) Drive adapter

gdpins_fake_drive.Rd

Simulates Google Drive using the local filesystem. State (including a trash store) is held in a mutable environment so all closures share it. Used as the default test seam — never hits the network.

Usage

gdpins_fake_drive(root = tempfile("gdpins_fake_drive_"))

Arguments

root

Character scalar. Root directory for the fake drive. Defaults to a fresh tempfile() path (created on first use).

Value

An object of class gdpins_drive_adapter.

Details

gd_trash() moves entries to an internal trash store (recoverable). It never calls unlink(). gd_md5() uses tools::md5sum(). gd_ls() excludes trashed entries.

This adapter is a test seam, not an offline-mode substitute: it starts from an empty tempdir with no relationship to any previously connected Drive folder, so swapping it in for gdpins_real_drive() on an already-connected board or raw connection will not preserve the existing Drive-side layout (create-confirm prompts/errors are the usual symptom). To temporarily disconnect an existing board or connection from Drive and work locally, use gdpins_go_offline() / gdpins_go_online() instead.

See also

gdpins_real_drive() for the live Google Drive adapter, gdpins_go_offline() to temporarily work offline on a real board or connection.

Examples

adapter <- gdpins_fake_drive()
adapter$mkdir("project/data-raw")
adapter$exists("project/data-raw")
#> [1] TRUE
